name: Programming Environment Spawned by a Suspicious Process
notes: This rule triggers when a programming environment is spawned by a suspicious process. This could indicate an attacker trying to execute a malicious script.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES '(cmd|powershell|rundll32|scrcons|(w|c)script|(ba)?sh)\.exe' AQL filter query

when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '(spoolsv|mshta|wscript|wuapp|taskeng|rundll32|regsvr32|iexplore|winword|powerpnt|excel|access|notepad|outlook|calc|iexplore|mspub|psexesvc|wsmprovhost|wmiprvse|visio|acrord\d\d)\.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Programming Environment Spawned by a Suspicious Process on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES '(cmd|powershell|rundll32|scrcons|(w|c)script|(ba)?sh)\.exe' AQL filter query

AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '(spoolsv|mshta|wscript|wuapp|taskeng|rundll32|regsvr32|iexplore|winword|powerpnt|excel|access|notepad|outlook|calc|iexplore|mspub|psexesvc|wsmprovhost|wmiprvse|visio|acrord\d\d)\.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Programming Environment Spawned by a Suspicious Process
Event description: Detects when a programming environment is spawned by a suspicious process.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
